title: Examples
text:
Recreate this plot
  
    Loading plot…
    
    
  
  Code
  
    
    library(ggplot2)

ggplot(penguins, aes(bill_len, bill_dep, colour = species)) +
  geom_point(na.rm = TRUE)
  
  
    Run
    Compare
      
         Side by side
         Overlay
         Wipe
      
    
  
  
  





  Recreate this plot
  
    Loading plot…
    
    
  
  Code
  
    
    tinyplot::tinyplot(dist ~ speed, data = cars)
  
  
    Run
    Compare
      
         Side by side
         Overlay
         Wipe

    Loading plot…

xyplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)

    Loading plot…

    library(plotly)
plot_ly(data = iris, x = ~Sepal.Length, y = ~Petal.Length, type = 'scatter')
  
  
    Run
    Compare
      
         Side by side
         Overlay
         Wipe

title: Examples
text:
matplotlib

  Recreate this plot
  
    Loading plot…
    
    
  
  Code
  
    
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
iris = load_iris()
data = iris.data
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.scatter(data[:, 0], data[:, 2])
  
  
    Run
    Compare
      
         Side by side
         Overlay
         Wipe

plotnine

    Loading plot…
    
    
  
  Code
  
    
    from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_point
from plotnine.data import mtcars
ggplot(mtcars, aes("wt", "mpg")) + geom_point()
  
  
    Run
    Compare
      
         Side by side

title: Examples
text:
seaborn

  Recreate this plot
  
    Loading plot…
    
    
  
  Code
  
    
    from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)
df['species'] = iris.target
sns.scatterplot(data=df, x="sepal length (cm)", y="petal length (cm)")
  
  
    Run
    Compare
      
         Side by side
         Overlay
         Wipe

title: PlotCat
text: PlotCat turns a plotting chunk into an exercise. Students see the finished plot, write R or Python in the browser, and compare their SVG with the target.

title: PlotCat
text:
Install
Add the extension to a Quarto project:
quarto add VisruthSK/PlotCat
Then enable the filter in a document or project config:
format:
  html:
    fig-format: svg

filters:
  - plotcat
R exercises use WebR. Python exercises use Pyodide. Both runtimes load after a student clicks Run.

title: PlotCat
text:
Write an exercise
Wrap one target chunk in a .plotcat Div.
::: {.plotcat}
```{r}
plot(cars)
```
:::
Add a second chunk for starter code. Mark it eval: false so Quarto does not run it while rendering the page.
::: {.plotcat}
```{r}
plot(cars, main = "Stopping distance")
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
plot(cars)
```
:::
The first chunk produces the target SVG during quarto render. PlotCat removes its source from the HTML. The optional second chunk appears in the editor but does not run during rendering.
